title: New resources to tackle unregulated immigration advice
body: "The Immigration Advice Authority (IAA) has updated its range of promotional materials to help advice seekers understand why regulated immigration advice matters, the risks of unregulated advice, and how to report poor or illegal advice.   As part of this update, the IAA has introduced a leaflet and two posters in three additional languages: Tigrinya, Amharic, and Traditional Chinese, making the resources even more accessible to diverse communities.   The materials provide clear guidance on:     how to check if an immigration adviser is registered and authorised to give advice    the risks posed by unregulated advisers    how to report concerns about poor advice or illegal activities     These resources are designed for use by regulated organisations, ambassadors, charities, and legal professionals to help ensure individuals receive reliable advice and avoid exploitation.   The IAA encourages organisations to download and share these materials widely to protect advice seekers from the risks of unregulated advice.   To download the materials or find out more, visit the IAA promotional materials page. "
